citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
Each research facility must establish at least one Committee. The CEO of this research facility has not yet appointed an
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) and therefore, two active protocols, SOP019 (24 hamsters used
November to December 2025), and SOP018 (20 ferrets used March to April 2026) have not been reviewed and approved
by an IACUC. An IACUC is necessary to review and approve the proposed activities for the care and use of animals and
to provide the proper oversight of the care and use of animals by the research facility. A properly constituted IACUC is
required of every Class R facility, regardless of protocol activity. The Chief Executive Officer of this research facility shall
appoint an IACUC, which shall include at a minimum a chairperson, the attending veterinarian and a person unaffiliated
with the facility to assess the animal program, facilities and procedures for the health and welfare of the animals.
Correct by: 06/01/2026.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
An Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) has not reviewed the research facility's Program for Animal
Care and Use or inspected the animal facilities at least once every 6 months as required under the provisions of the
Animal Welfare Act (AWA). Regular reviews are necessary to ensure adequate IACUC oversight and to ensure the
welfare of the animals being used in research by the facility. Reports of those reviews must be prepared and submitted to
the Institutional Official every 6 months. The facility must ensure that semiannual inspections and program reviews are
completed at least once every 6 months.
Correct by: 07/01/2026.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
There has been no review and approval of animal activities being performed before animals were used on Protocols
SOP018 (20 ferrets from March to April 2026) and SOP019 (24 hamsters from November to December 2025) by an
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). IACUC review and approval of animal use is necessary to ensure
that activities involving animals are being conducted in accordance with the Animal Welfare Act. The IACUC should make
certain that all activities relating to the care and use of animals receive adequate oversight and that the activities are in
accordance with the IACUC reviewed and approved protocols, SOP's and policies.
Correct by 07/01/2026.

The research facility did not submit their USDA fiscal year 2025 (FY25) Annual Report to the Deputy Administrator on or
before the extended deadline due date of 12/31/25. Per this Section, research facilities that use or intend to use live
animals in research, tests, experiments, or for teaching shall submit an annual report, that covers the numbers of animals
used during the previous Federal fiscal year, to the Deputy Administrator on or before the December due date in this
Subchapter of the AWA. Timely submission of the annual report is essential to provide an accurate accounting of the total
number of animals used and to ensure that professionally acceptable standards governing the care and use of animals
were followed during the previous Federal fiscal year reporting period. The research facility needs to submit their USDA
FY25 Annual Report.
Correct by: 07/01/2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(a) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
Each research facility must establish at least one Committee. The CEO of this research facility has not yet appointed an 
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) and therefore, two active protocols, SOP019 (24 hamsters used 
November to December 2025), and SOP018 (20 ferrets used March to April 2026) have not been reviewed and approved 
by an IACUC. An IACUC is necessary to review and approve the proposed activities for the care and use of animals and 
to provide the proper oversight of the care and use of animals by the research facility. A properly constituted IACUC is 
required of every Class R facility, regardless of protocol activity. The Chief Executive Officer of this research facility shall 
appoint an IACUC, which shall include at a minimum a chairperson, the attending veterinarian and a person unaffiliated 
with the facility to assess the animal program, facilities and procedures for the health and welfare of the animals.  
Correct by: 06/01/2026. 
2.31(c) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
An Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) has not reviewed the research facility's Program for Animal 
Care and Use or inspected the animal facilities at least once every 6 months as required under the provisions of the 
Animal Welfare Act (AWA). Regular reviews are necessary to ensure adequate IACUC oversight and to ensure the 
welfare of the animals being used in research by the facility. Reports of those reviews must be prepared and submitted to 
the Institutional Official every 6 months. The facility must ensure that semiannual inspections and program reviews are

2.31(d) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
There has been no review and approval of animal activities being performed before animals were used on Protocols 
SOP018 (20 ferrets from March to April 2026) and SOP019 (24 hamsters from November to December 2025) by an 
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). IACUC review and approval of animal use is necessary to ensure 
that activities involving animals are being conducted in accordance with the Animal Welfare Act. The IACUC should make 
certain that all activities relating to the care and use of animals receive adequate oversight and that the activities are in

accordance with the IACUC reviewed and approved protocols, SOP's and policies.  
Correct by 07/01/2026. 
2.36(a) 
   
Annual report. 
The research facility did not submit their USDA fiscal year 2025 (FY25) Annual Report to the Deputy Administrator on or 
before the extended deadline due date of 12/31/25. Per this Section, research facilities that use or intend to use live 
animals in research, tests, experiments, or for teaching shall submit an annual report, that covers the numbers of animals 
used during the previous Federal fiscal year, to the Deputy Administrator on or before the December due date in this 
Subchapter of the AWA. Timely submission of the annual report is essential to provide an accurate accounting of the total 
number of animals used and to ensure that professionally acceptable standards governing the care and use of animals 
were followed during the previous Federal fiscal year reporting period. The research facility needs to submit their USDA 
FY25 Annual Report.  
Correct by: 07/01/2026.
